data.title: Trump targets FHA for massive layoffs, with plans for at least 40% cull - report
data.description:
The Trump administration is set to reduce the Federal Housing Administration's workforce by at least 40%, Bloomberg reported on Tuesday, citing two sources familiar with the plans.

Known as one of the world's largest mortgage insurers, the FHA helps people who can't afford big down payments or have lower credit scores to get home loans by insuring those mortgages.

As such, the planned FHA layoffs might reduce the agency's ability to process and approve mortgage insurance, and, consequently, make it harder for low-income, prospective homebuyers to secure financing.

The news comes as thousands of federal staffers have been let go by the Trump administration over the past few days. The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp., for instance, was said to have fired an unspecified number of probationary employees on Monday, following voluntary buyouts under the White House's federal-workforce-reduction efforts that have already reduced the top banking regulator's workforce.

The U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Affairs, which oversees the FHA, intends to reduce its staff by half, Bloomberg Law previously reported. The agency employs 9,600 people, its website showed, although it's unclear how many employees are specifically assigned to FHA duties.

data.title: Ecopetrol raised to Buy at Citi as election trade looks to have started early
data.description:
Ecopetrol (NYSE:EC) +5.4% in Tuesday's trading as Citi upgrades to Buy/High Risk from Neutral/High Risk with a $14 price target, raised from $9, seeing considerable room for a valuation re-rating even as politics remain a key concern ahead of presidential elections due next year.

Equity investors appear to have seen recent political events including the U.S.-Colombia tariff-related incident and the recently televisied presidential cabinet meeting as an early trigger to engage in the "election trade," and while such a trade was anticipated sometime in 2025, it seems to be playing out more quickly than expected, Citi analyst Andres Cardona says.

The analyst restores Ecopetrol's (NYSE:EC) probable reserves case into his core net assetvalue calculations, believing new ooil exploration bidding rounds likely would occur in Colombia under a new administration.

But until the election, Cardona notes the company's challenge in stabilizing declining production in Colombia given President Petro's mandate to make no new oil exploration bidding rounds, which limits mid-to-long-term development opportunities.

data.title: Bill Ackman to announce transformational deal at market close on Tuesday
data.description:
Bill Ackman, head of hedge fund Pershing Square Capital Management, said he'll announce a transformational deal, akin to Warren Buffett gaining control of Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) (BRK.A), a struggling textiles manufacturer, in 1965.

"At 4pm, we are going to announce a potential transaction which, if completed, will provide me and my firm with the opportunity to create our own, you might say, modern-day version of Berkshire," Ackman said in a post on X. "Fortunately, our starting base of assets won't be a dying textile company, but a very good business."

He highlighted Buffett's strategy of directing cash generated by the Berkshire textile operations into an insurance company, a bank, manufacturing businesses, a railroad, and a securities portfolio,

"We will adopt similar, long-term, shareholder-oriented principles to Berkshire, and we intend to hold the stock forever," Ackman added.

He'll make a presentation and hold a Q&A session on X on Wednesday at 9 AM ET.

Ackman's publicly traded investment company is Pershing Square Holdings (OTCPK:PSHZF).

data.title: Tri Pointe Homes targets 5,500-6,100 home deliveries in 2025 amid market expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Tri Pointe Homes (NYSE:TPH) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Doug Bauer highlighted that Tri Pointe delivered 1,748 new homes in Q4 2024, generating $1.2 billion in home sales revenue. The company achieved a gross margin of 23.3% during the quarter and recorded $129 million in net income, or $1.37 per diluted share. For the full year, Tri Pointe set a record with 6,460 home deliveries and $4.83 in diluted EPS.
  • The company redeemed $450 million in senior notes, significantly strengthening the balance sheet. A 14.5% return on average equity for the year was noted, supported by disciplined capital allocation and share repurchases.
  • CEO Bauer emphasized progress in geographic diversification, with significant delivery gains in Texas and the Carolinas, alongside strategic expansions into Salt Lake City, Orlando, and Coastal Carolinas.
  • CFO Glenn Keeler stated, "Home sales revenue was $1.2 billion for the quarter with an average sales price of $699,000. Gross margins were 23.3% for the quarter, right at the midpoint of our guidance."

Outlook

  • For Q1 2025, Tri Pointe anticipates delivering between 900 and 1,100 homes at an average sales price of $685,000 to $695,000, with gross margins projected between 22% and 23%.
  • For the full year 2025, the company projects deliveries between 5,500 and 6,100 homes, with an average sales price of $660,000 to $670,000. Gross margins are expected in the range of 20.5% to 22%, with an SG&A expense ratio of 11% to 12%.
  • Management acknowledged potential headwinds from macroeconomic factors, including political uncertainties, but expressed confidence in long-term market fundamentals, supported by strong demographics and a housing supply shortage.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 adjusted EPS was $1.37, supported by strong home sales revenue of $1.2 billion. The company delivered 1,748 homes during the quarter, achieving a gross margin of 23.3%.
  • SG&A expenses as a percentage of home sales revenue improved to 10.3%, contributing to a pre-tax margin of 14%.
  • The company's cash position reached $970 million, with a total liquidity of $1.7 billion. Homebuilding debt-to-capital ratio stood at 21.6%, an all-time low.
  • Share repurchases totaled $50 million in Q4, with a new $250 million authorization announced for 2025.

Q&A

  • Stephen Kim, Evercore ISI: Asked about what factors could lead to the lower end of gross margin guidance for 2025. CFO Glenn Keeler responded, "That would imply continued elevated incentives like we saw in the first quarter."
  • Paul Przybylski, Wolfe Research: Asked about pricing power and supply chain impacts. CEO Doug Bauer noted that pricing power varies by market, ranging between 1% to 5%, while the company has strategies to manage insurance challenges in entry-level markets.
  • Mike Dahl, RBC Capital Markets: Inquired about demand trends and the strategic decision to lower absorption targets. COO Tom Mitchell explained, "We’re making a conscious effort and planning our absorption at about three per month, focusing on enhancing margins."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of margins amid elevated incentives and macroeconomic uncertainties. The tone was slightly skeptical but balanced with recognition of the company’s strategic focus.
  • Management exhibited confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing strong fundamentals and strategic priorities. However, during exchanges with analysts, responses reflected caution and a focus on managing near-term headwinds.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone was slightly more cautious, particularly regarding short-term demand fluctuations and macroeconomic uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 deliveries increased to 1,748 homes from 1,619 in Q3. However, cancellations rose slightly to 14% compared to 10% in Q3.
  • Gross margins remained consistent at 23.3% quarter-over-quarter, but incentives increased to 7% in Q4 from 5.5% in Q3.
  • The strategic focus shifted toward maintaining margins and managing inventory, with a deliberate slowdown in community openings to align with anticipated spring demand in 2025.

Risks and Concerns

  • Elevated mortgage rates and ongoing political uncertainties were noted as potential headwinds impacting consumer confidence.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the company’s ability to sustain margins amid elevated incentives and the competitive landscape in certain markets.
  • Management acknowledged challenges in some markets, such as higher inventory levels in Austin, but emphasized their premium positioning and strategic flexibility.

Final Takeaway

Tri Pointe Homes concluded 2024 with record-setting performance in home deliveries and profitability, while emphasizing strategic growth in new markets like Salt Lake City and Orlando. Despite macroeconomic headwinds, the company remains confident in its ability to navigate short-term challenges and capitalize on long-term growth opportunities, with a focus on maintaining margins and disciplined capital allocation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Gilead opts out of deal for Arcus cancer therapy
data.description:
Shares of Arcus Biosciences (NYSE:RCUS) hit a new 52-week low on Tuesday after the biotech said that Gilead Sciences’ (NASDAQ:GILD) option rights to partner with the company for a potential anticancer agent called casdatifan have expired.

Hayward, California-headquartered Arcus (NYSE:RCUS) noted that, as a result, the company retains rights to the therapy, which was subject to a 10-year partnership initiated between the company and Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) in 2020.

Casdatifan is a small molecule designed to target the cancer-causing agent HIF-2a. Noting a recent data readout at a medical conference and possibly with Merck’s (MRK) Welireg in mind, RCUS CEO Terry Rosen highlighted casdatifan’s potential to become “a best-in-class HIF-2a inhibitor in what appears to be a two-horse race.”

On Tuesday, the New Jersey-based pharma giant announced the EU approval for its oral HIF-2a inhibitor as a single agent for two cancer conditions linked to kidneys.

“We are thrilled to retain ownership of casdatifan, which has the potential to address a significant unmet need for patients with an estimated $5 billion market opportunity,” Rosen added.

data.title: Capital One, Discover shareholders approve deal
data.description:
Capital One Financial (NYSE:COF) and Discover Financial Services (NYSE:DFS) holders approved the planned $35 billion purchase of Discover.

The shareholders of each company approved the deal in separate votes on Tuesday, according to a statement.

Capital One (NYSE:COF) expects the deal will close early this year, subject to approval by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, and other customary closing conditions.

data.title: Beyond Meat jumps to its 2025 high amid interest in its seared steak tips product
data.description:
Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) was one of the biggest gainers in the consumer sector on Tuesday, with a gain of 11.2%.

The company did not release any press releases or SEC filings to account for the buying action. However, Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) was profiled favorably in US Magazine in an article titled "Kim Kardashian and A-List Athletes Are Obsessed with This Plant-Based Meat Alternative." The publication highlighted that Beyond Seared Steak Tips are particularly popular with the jet set.

Google Search trends also confirm a recent spike in consumer interest in the number of queries related to Beyond Meat's Seared Steak Tips.

The rally pushed Beyond Meat (BYND) to its highest trading level of the year at $4.39 a share earlier in the session. However, the plant-based meat stock is still far below the 52-week high of $12.12 or the all-time high of $234.90 in 2019. Short interest on Beyond Meat (BYND) stands at 48% of the total float.

data.title: Silver Lake nears deal for stake in Intel's Altera
data.description:
Private equity firm Silver Lake Management is in exclusive discussions to purchase a majority stake in Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) Altera unit.

The PE firm is working out details of an investment in the Altera programmable chip unit, according to a Bloomerg report on Tuesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The exact size of the stake hasn't been decided.

Intel and Silver Lake declined to comment to Bloomberg.

Shares of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) jumped 14% on Tuesday after reports that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Broadcom (AVGO) could each look to acquire parts of the company.

data.title: CNX Resources raised at Raymond James as 'rising tide lifts all boats'
data.description:
CNX Resources (NYSE:CNX) +1.8% in Tuesday's trading as Raymond James upgrades to Market Perform from Underperform, believing the company will benefit from the "rising tide lifts all boats theme," assuming the firm's bullish natural gas outlook proves accurate.

Ray Jay analyst John Freeman says his biggest issue with CNX Resources (NYSE:CNX) is the company's hedges limit its ability to participate in the bullish natural gas outlook, but if the firm's strong outlook proves accurate, he believes the company will benefit from the "rising tide lifts all boats" theme.

The company admittedly will lag its gassy peers with more exposure to the strip, but Freeman says the stock no longer warrants an outright sell rating, anticipating an in-line performance compared to the broader market as its distributable cash flow gets dragged higher by an improving gas strip.

CNX (CNX) has lagged its gassy peers by ~40% over the past three months since natural gas prices began their big rally, with some of the sizzle taken out of the stock from the New Technologies segment following the 45V ruling at the start of the year along with the onerous hedge position, Freeman adds.

data.title: IHG targets 4%+ net system growth and launches $900M buyback for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: InterContinental Hotels Group PLC (NYSE:IHG) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Elie Maalouf highlighted 2024 as a year of strong financial performance, emphasizing 4.3% net system growth and a $900 million share buyback program for 2025. The company also finalized the acquisition of the Ruby brand for $116 million, expanding its presence in the premium urban lifestyle segment.
  • IHG added 59,000 rooms to its portfolio, taking the total to 987,000 across more than 6,600 hotels. Signings reached 106,000 rooms, increasing the pipeline to over 2,200 hotels.
  • CFO Michael Glover reported fee business revenue growth of 6% and a 190-basis-point increase in fee margin to 61.2%. Adjusted EPS grew 15%, supported by the $800 million share buyback completed in 2024.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its 2025 target of over 4% net system growth, excluding the 0.7% impact from the end of the Venetian agreement.
  • The Ruby acquisition is expected to generate $8 million in incremental fee revenue by 2028, with franchise fees anticipated to exceed $15 million annually by 2030.
  • Future leverage is projected to remain at the lower end of the 2.5x to 3x net debt-to-EBITDA range by the end of 2025.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for 2024 totaled $2.3 billion, with EBIT reaching $1.124 billion, reflecting a 10% year-over-year increase.
  • RevPAR grew 3% for the year, with Q4 achieving 4.6%, driven by a combination of rate and occupancy gains.
  • Operating profit from reportable segments rose 10%, supported by positive trading performance and fee margin progression.

Q&A

  • Jamie Rollo, Morgan Stanley: Questioned the impact of higher key money and its revenue implications. CFO Glover clarified that the increase in key money, driven by Luxury & Lifestyle openings and conversions, would minimally impact fees, with a $5 million to $6 million effect expected in 2025.
  • Vicki Stern, Barclays: Asked about the competitive environment for key money and fee growth in China. CEO Maalouf emphasized that IHG maintains stable fee structures in China and expects high single-digit net unit growth in the region.
  • Jaina Mistry, Jefferies: Inquired about the profitability timeline for Ruby. CEO Maalouf indicated that integration costs in 2025 would prevent immediate accretion, but fee contributions are expected from 2026 onward.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts generally adopted a neutral tone, focusing on key money increases, RevPAR growth potential, and regional performance.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, highlighting achievements in system growth and shareholder returns. The Q&A session featured detailed responses, emphasizing strategic clarity.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent tone, reiterating confidence in long-term growth plans.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, reaffirming over 4% net system growth targets for 2025.
  • Strategic shifts included the Ruby acquisition and the launch of a $900 million share buyback program for 2025, compared to the previous $800 million program in 2024.
  • Management sentiment was steady, with continued emphasis on RevPAR growth and expansion in high-growth geographies.
  • Analysts’ tone remained focused on regional performance metrics, particularly China and the Americas.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged elevated key money levels, attributing the increase to strategic investments in Luxury & Lifestyle segments and conversions.
  • Dependency on global economic stability and geopolitical factors, particularly in regions like China and the Middle East, were noted as potential challenges.
  • Analysts expressed concerns regarding fee growth consistency in the Americas and regional impacts from outbound Chinese travel on ADRs.

Final Takeaway

IHG delivered robust financial performance in 2024, driven by significant system growth and strategic advancements such as the Ruby acquisition. With a $900 million share buyback program and over 4% net system growth targeted for 2025, the company reinforced its commitment to shareholder value. Expansion in premium urban lifestyle and high-growth geographies underpins confidence in sustained long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
